title: NOC 65229 – Other support occupations in personal services | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 65229 is a versatile category that includes the specialized support staff essential to Canada's personal care and funeral service industries. From weight-loss consultants and tanning salon attendants to funeral pallbearers and crematorium assistants, these TEER 5 professionals provide high-touch services that support the daily lives and transitions of Canadians. In 2026, as the "Wellness Economy" and personalized service sectors expand, this role offers a unique and accessible entry point for international workers. For immigration, this occupation is frequently utilized in Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) and the Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP) to address labor needs in specialized service niches.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level5Major Group65 – Sales and service support occupationsSub-Major Group652 – Support occupations in food, accommodation and tourismMinor Group6522 – Support occupations in personal servicesNOC Code65229

duties: Wellness/Weight Loss: Consult with clients to provide information on weight loss programs, track progress, and sell health-related products.
Funeral Services: Assist at funeral services by acting as a pallbearer, coordinating floral arrangements, and providing support to grieving families.
Crematoriums: Operate equipment to cremate remains and ensure all legal and ethical protocols are followed.
Personal Care: Provide services such as tanning assistance, shoe shining, or managing locker room facilities in sports clubs or hotels.
General: Process payments, maintain client records, and ensure the cleanliness of the service facility.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided for all specific service and safety protocols.
Some roles, such as crematorium assistants, may require specialized provincial certifications or licensing.
High proficiency in English or French (or both) is essential for effective client consultation and sensitive communication.
A professional, empathetic, and customer-focused attitude is highly valued by Canadian employers.
job_outlook: The outlook for this occupation is Favourable. Projections indicate a steady increase in job openings driven by the growth of the personalized wellness sector and the natural replacement of retiring workers in the funeral services industry. As Canadians continue to prioritize convenience and specialized personal care, the need for dedicated support staff in these niche markets will remain a priority for the service sector.
labour_market_demand: The demand for other personal service support occupations is Stable across Canada, with localized "Hot Spots" in major urban centers. In 2026, there is a noted surge in demand for Consultants in Weight Loss and Wellness Centers in Ontario and British Columbia, reflecting a national trend toward health-conscious lifestyles. Additionally, the funeral services sector continues to see steady demand for support staff in provinces with aging populations, such as Nova Scotia and New Brunswick.
High Accessibility: Minimal formal education requirements allow you to secure employment and begin your Canadian career quickly.
Specialized Skill Sets: Gain unique experience in consultation, customer health tracking, or specialized service protocols.
Compassionate Work Environments: Ideal for those who value interpersonal connection and providing support during significant life events.
Path to PR: Accumulate the necessary Canadian work experience to qualify for "Semi-Skilled" immigration streams in high-demand regions.
pathways: Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion route for TEER 5 workers with job offers from designated employers in the Atlantic region.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): A dedicated pathway for those working in the personal service sector for at least 9 months in BC.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track PR for those currently working in Alberta on a valid work permit in eligible service categories.
Manitoba Skilled Worker Stream: Accessible for those who have worked in the province for at least 6 months.
